title: rosa × dumetorum f. collina
intro: Rosa × dumetorum f. collina, also known as wild rose or briar rose, is a type of shrub that belongs to the rose family and is characterized by its fragrant flowers and thorny stems. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, due to its high content of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rosa × dumetorum f. collina include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and improve overall well-being. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in skincare products for its antiseptic and anti-aging properties.
